author: g5n-dev
title: Global State + LRU Cache Threading Hazard in cli.py
notes: Central claim that @lru_cache is not thread-safe is factually wrong — CPython's lru_cache uses an internal lock and is thread-safe. Cache invalidation fires on register_detector() which always adds a new detector name, so invalidation is correct and necessary. This is a CLI entry point, not a web server — multi-threading concerns are theoretical. The _DetectorNamesCacheCompat is explicitly labeled a compat shim for tests. Redundant caching layers is a minor observation, not poor engineering.
